global.title: Operation batches stored at /bytes carry full OperationWithContext entries including signer signatures, preserving authorship on recovery.
global.content:
## Batch payload shape

Each operation batch uploaded to `/bytes` is an encrypted JSON array of `OperationWithContext`:

```
{
  operation: {
    id: "c6401aec-...",
    index: 0,
    skip: 0,
    timestampUtcMs: "2026-04-11T18:06:34.210Z",
    hash: "gfgqmD...",
    action: {
      type: "CREATE_DOCUMENT",
      input: { name: "push", model: "powerhouse/document-model", ... },
      scope: "document",
      context: { signer: { user: { address: "0xadbA7C2F..." } } }
    }
  },
  context: {
    documentId: "57033542-...",
    documentType: "powerhouse/document-model",
    scope: "document",
    branch: "main",
    ordinal: 5
  }
}
```

## Why this shape matters

- `operation.id` is stable across replays — recovery produces the same op IDs.
- `action.context.signer.signatures` is preserved verbatim, so authorship stays intact across device migration.
- `context.ordinal` is the global ordinal from `operation_index_operations`, enabling deterministic ordering even when batches arrive out of order.
- The reference address is a **content hash** of the ciphertext, so identical batches deduplicate naturally on Swarm.
global.description: Each encrypted batch contains an array of OperationWithContext with the action, its scope, timestamp, hash, signer context, plus documentId, branch, and ordinal metadata.
